data.content:
epic hero,protagonist	Odysseus
Odyssey
Odysseus is the central hero of the Odyssey.

island setting,residence	Calypso
Ogygia
Calypso lives on the island of Ogygia.

detention,love,mythological relationship,offer of immortality,romantic relationship	Calypso
Odysseus
Calypso is described as loving Odysseus and yearning for his return.<SEP>Calypso detains Odysseus on Ogygia for seven years, offers him immortality, and in some accounts bears him children.

literary depiction,myth transmission	Calypso
Homer
Homer includes Calypso as a character in the Odyssey.

Greek epic,narrative role	Calypso
Odyssey
Calypso's detention of Odysseus is a key episode in the Odyssey.

divine power,gift	Calypso
Immortality
Calypso promises to grant Odysseus immortality if he stays with her.

mythological figure,pantheon	Calypso
Greek Mythology
Calypso is a nymph within Greek Mythology.

mythic family,parent-child	Pleiades
Pleione
Pleione is the mother of the Pleiades.

etymology,name meaning	Calypso
She Who Conceals
Calypso's name translates as 'she who conceals'.

interpretation,name meaning	Calypso
Concealing the Knowledge
Etymologicum Magnum interprets Calypso's name as 'concealing the knowledge'.

marital relationship,parenthood	Oceanus
Tethys
Tethys and Oceanus together are parents of the Oceanid nymphs.

etymology,lexical analysis	Calypso
Etymologicum Magnum
Etymologicum Magnum explains the meaning of Calypso's name.

mythic genealogy,parent-child	Oceanid Nymphs
Tethys
Tethys is mother to the Oceanid nymphs, including a Calypso in some accounts.

characterization,epithet	Calypso
Dolóessa
The Homeric epithet dolóessa, meaning subtle or wily, is applied to Calypso to justify her reclusive character.

mythic genealogy,parent-child	Oceanid Nymphs
Oceanus
Oceanus is father to the Oceanid nymphs, including a Calypso in some accounts.

divine lineage,parent-child	Atlas
Calypso
Calypso is generally said to be the daughter of the Titan Atlas.

marital relationship,parenthood	Doris
Nereus
Nereus and Doris together are the parents of the Nereids.

alternative lineage,parent-child	Calypso
Pleione
In the Fabulae, Pleione is presented as the mother of Calypso.

mythic genealogy,parent-child	Nereids
Nereus
Nereus is father to the Nereids, which include Calypso in some accounts.

classification,textual mention	Calypso
Hesiod
Hesiod mentions a Calypso as one of the Oceanid nymphs.

mythic genealogy,parent-child	Doris
Nereids
Doris is mother to the Nereids, which include Calypso in some accounts.

marital relationship,parenthood	Helios
Perse
Helios and Perse together are parents of Calypso and Circe in John Tzetzes's account.

classification,textual mention	Calypso
Homeric Hymn to Demeter
The anonymous author of the Homeric Hymn to Demeter mentions a Calypso as an Oceanid nymph.

divine lineage,parent-child	Circe
Helios
Helios is also the father of Circe.

group membership,sea nymph	Calypso
Oceanid Nymphs
Some sources classify Calypso among the Oceanid nymphs.

divine lineage,parent-child	Circe
Perse
Perse is also the mother of Circe.

classification,textual mention	Apollodorus
Calypso
Apollodorus includes Calypso in his list of Nereids.

group membership,sea nymph	Calypso
Nereids
Calypso is listed among the Nereids in Apollodorus's account.

parent-child,people origin	Cephalonians
Hermes
Hermes is said to have fathered the Cephalonians with Calypso.

genealogical claim,interpretation	Calypso
John Tzetzes
John Tzetzes claims that Calypso is a daughter of Helios and Perse.

emotional bond,marriage	Odysseus
Penelope
Odysseus is married to Penelope and longs to return to her while detained by Calypso.

divine lineage,parent-child	Calypso
Helios
Helios is presented as the father of Calypso by John Tzetzes.

patronage,protection	Athena
Odysseus
Athena is the patron goddess of Odysseus and advocates for his release from Calypso.

command,divine messenger	Hermes
Zeus
Zeus orders Hermes to tell Calypso to set Odysseus free.

divine lineage,parent-child	Calypso
Perse
Perse is presented as the mother of Calypso by John Tzetzes.

divine hierarchy,petition	Athena
Zeus
Athena asks Zeus to order Odysseus's release from Calypso's island.

character similarity,sibling relationship	Calypso
Circe
Calypso and Circe are both presented as children of Helios and Perse and are sometimes confused due to similar behavior and ties to Odysseus.

divine will,fate determination	Odysseus
Zeus
Zeus declares that it is not Odysseus's destiny to live with Calypso forever and orders his release.

genealogy,textual mention	Calypso
Catalogue of Women
A fragment from the Catalogue of Women states that Calypso bore the Cephalonians to Hermes.

alternative tradition,parent-child	Latinus
Odysseus
Some later accounts attribute Latinus as a son of Odysseus by Calypso.

parent-child,people origin	Calypso
Cephalonians
Calypso is said to have borne the Cephalonians.

mythic offspring,parent-child	Nausithous
Odysseus
In some accounts, Odysseus is the father of Nausithous by Calypso.

alternative parentage,myth variation	Circe
Latinus
Circe is usually given as Latinus's mother rather than Calypso.

mythic offspring,parent-child	Nausinous
Odysseus
In some accounts, Odysseus is the father of Nausinous by Calypso.

divine encounter,message delivery	Calypso
Hermes
Hermes visits Calypso's island in the Odyssey and in one fragment fathers children with her.

alternative tradition,parent-child	Calypso
Latinus
Some later accounts say that Calypso bore Odysseus a son, Latinus.

hero archetype,narrative comparison	Gilgamesh
Odysseus
The story of Odysseus and Calypso is compared to interactions between Gilgamesh and Siduri.

parent-child,sea god lineage	Polyphemus
Poseidon
Polyphemus is a cyclops son of Poseidon.

ancient epic,protagonist	Epic of Gilgamesh
Gilgamesh
Gilgamesh is the central hero of the Epic of Gilgamesh.

mythic offspring,parent-child	Calypso
Nausithous
In some accounts, Calypso bears Nausithous to Odysseus.

mythic offspring,parent-child	Calypso
Nausinous
In some accounts, Calypso bears Nausinous to Odysseus.

divine punishment,enmity	Odysseus

Poseidon detests Odysseus because Odysseus blinded his son Polyphemus.

group membership,mythic creature	Cyclops
Polyphemus

character inclusion,mythic narrative	Epic of Gilgamesh

Letitia Landon

female figure,narrative comparison	Calypso
Siduri
Calypso's dealings with Odysseus parallel Siduri's dealings with Gilgamesh.

Ryan Patrick Hanley

detention motive,favour	Calypso
Poseidon

Odysseus
The poem describes Calypso as endlessly yearning for Odysseus and critiques such obsession.

island,setting	Ogygia

divine messenger,plot catalyst	Hermes

authorship,mythological account,textual report,tragic end	Calypso
Hyginus
Hyginus reports that Calypso killed herself.<SEP>Hyginus states that Calypso killed herself because of her love for Odysseus.

advocacy,divine intervention	Athena
The Odyssey (Calypso Episode)
In this episode Athena advocates to Zeus for Odysseus's release.

emotional depiction,literary portrayal	Calypso
Calypso Watching the Ocean
The poem Calypso Watching the Ocean portrays Calypso as eternally yearning for Odysseus's return.

commentary,interpretation,philosophical analysis,philosophical interpretation	Calypso
Ryan Patrick Hanley
Ryan Patrick Hanley comments on the interpretation of Calypso and her story.<SEP>Ryan Patrick Hanley comments on interpretations of Calypso in Les Aventures de Télé.

decision making,divine authority	The Odyssey (Calypso Episode)
Zeus
In this episode Zeus decides that Odysseus must be freed from Calypso.

central figure,plot element	Calypso

Calypso is the central figure in the Odyssey episode where she detains Odysseus.

semantic meaning,term clarification	Dolóessa
Dolóessa (Epithet)
Dolóessa (epithet) represents the Homeric term dolóessa, which means subtle or wily.

assistance,provisioning	Calypso
Odysseus's Boat
Calypso provides Odysseus with tools, materials, and supplies to build and provision a boat for his departure.

divinely ordered,release	Calypso
Odysseus's Departure
After seven years and the intervention of the gods, Calypso allows Odysseus to build a boat and leave her island.

mythological biography,subject coverage	Calypso
Calypso (Mythology)
The article Calypso (mythology) presents detailed information about the nymph Calypso.

destination comparison,hero journey	Honoured People
Odysseus
